title: Pedicularis orthocoryne
taxonomy: Pedicularis orthocoryne is classified as a member of the family Orobanchaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as broomrapes. This classification places it within the order Lamiales, a large and diverse order of flowering plants. Within the family Orobanchaceae, Pedicularis orthocoryne is further classified as a species of the genus Pedicularis, which contains about 600 species of flowering plants. The genus Pedicularis is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as louseworts or woodland-gentians. They are primarily found in the mountainous regions of the Northern Hemisphere.

intro: Pedicularis orthocoryne, also known as the "Indian paintbrush," is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae, commonly found in the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pedicularis orthocoryne include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or powder, and is used to make remedies for treating coughs, colds, and sore throats, as well as to aid in digestion and boost the immune system.
